claim_map.claim_text: Loop engineering can be framed in this chapter as designing the repeatable harness around an agent, including triggers, goals, context, tools, checks, state, reports, retries, and escalation.
The chapter should avoid presenting loop engineering as proof that prompts have disappeared; the safer framing is that prompts may be one component inside a broader operating loop.

forbidden_claims_checked.forbidden_claim: Prompt engineering has been replaced by loop engineering across the industry.
Prompts have disappeared from agent systems.
The supplied evidence proves production effectiveness or safety of any specific loop architecture.
Loop engineering is a universally accepted standard term with settled definitions.
Hermes is a runtime dependency of OpenClaw.
Hermes is the general operating environment for OpenClaw.
OpenClaw requires Hermes for web or phone access.
Migration/setup/import tooling adjacency proves architectural dependency.

findings.finding: All proposed claims map to evidence_refs and citation_map entries.
The target file suggestion is an allowed substantive canary target: docs/book/06-operating-loops.md.
The packet includes a narrow markdown delta suitable for guarded dry-run patching.
No human-review dependency field or term is introduced.
Disposition is within the allowed disposition list.

publish_packets.proposed_markdown_delta:
### Loop engineering as an operating harness

In this book, loop engineering should be treated as the design of the repeatable harness around an agent. That harness includes the event or schedule that starts the work, the goal the agent is trying to satisfy, the context and tools available to it, the checks that evaluate progress, the state carried between turns or runs, and the reporting, retry, and escalation paths that keep the loop bounded.

This framing keeps the chapter focused on operating behavior rather than on a claim that prompting has been replaced. A prompt may still be part of the loop, but the publishable emphasis here is on the surrounding system: what starts the loop, what the agent may do, how results are checked, what is remembered, and when control is handed off or stopped.

publication_readiness.reason: The selected candidate is allowed_for_author, has contradiction_status not_found, uses three supplied source mappings, and supports only a bounded conceptual canary for docs/book/06-operating-loops.md. No deployment has been applied by this packet.

findings.finding: The packet uses exactly one substantive candidate and does not combine unrelated candidates into a broader unsupported claim.
The proposed prose is paraphrased and does not copy raw source text.
The packet avoids the known OpenClaw/Hermes overpromotion boundary from prior runs.
The evidence is moderate rather than strong, so the prose must remain conceptual and bounded to chapter framing rather than asserting settled practice or industry consensus.
No private, social-only, or raw capture content is exposed; the source privacy status is publishable metadata only.

required_caveats: Present loop engineering as a useful chapter framing, not as a settled industry-wide transition.
Do not claim prompts have disappeared or been replaced in all agent systems.
Do not broaden the claim beyond the supplied candidate's harness elements: triggers, goals, context, tools, checks, state, reports, retries, and escalation.
Do not reproduce raw source text; use only paraphrased book prose derived from the supplied evidence metadata.
Do not add OpenClaw/Hermes dependency, runtime, operating-environment, web-access, or phone-access claims.

The source metadata supports a narrow conceptual canary about loop-engineering harness design. It does not support stronger claims about settled industry transition, universal practice, replacement of prompting, or production guarantees.
publish_packets.summary: A single guarded canary is safe to propose for the operating-loops chapter. The proposed prose frames loop engineering narrowly as the design of the repeatable operating harness around an agent, using only the supplied existing evidence candidate and its mapped source metadata. It does not claim an industry-wide transition, does not say prompts have disappeared, and does not reproduce raw source text.
publish_packets.title: Loop engineering as the harness around an agent
